---
# =============================================================================
# blog/posts/quarto-setup/index.qmd — "Setting up Quarto" blog post
# =============================================================================
#
# HOW TO EDIT: see blog/posts/welcome/index.qmd for full instructions.
#
# =============================================================================
title: "Setting Up a Quarto Website for Your GitHub Organisation"
description: >
  A step-by-step guide to creating a Quarto-powered static site for a GitHub
  organisation, including automatic deployment with GitHub Actions.
author: "Jules G Org"
date: "2024-02-01"
categories:
  - quarto
  - github-pages
  - tutorial
image: "cover.png"
toc: true
---

## Introduction

[Quarto](https://quarto.org) makes it straightforward to publish a polished
organisation website directly from a GitHub repository.  This post walks
through the setup used for this very site.

## Prerequisites

- A GitHub account / organisation
- [Quarto CLI](https://quarto.org/docs/get-started/) installed locally
- Basic familiarity with Markdown

## Step 1 — Create the Repository

On GitHub, create a repository named `<org>.github.io` (for organisations) or
`<username>.github.io` (for personal sites).  This special naming convention
tells GitHub Pages to serve the repo at `https://<org>.github.io`.

For example:

```
Organisation: julesg-org
Repository:   julesg-org.github.io   ← served at https://julesg-org.github.io
```

## Step 2 — Initialise a Quarto Website

```bash
# In your cloned repository folder:
quarto create project website .
```

This creates the minimal `_quarto.yml` and `index.qmd` scaffold.

## Step 3 — Customise `_quarto.yml`

Open `_quarto.yml` and set at minimum:

```yaml
project:
  type: website

website:
  title: "Your Org Name"
  site-url: https://your-org.github.io
  navbar:
    left:
      - text: "Home"
        href: index.qmd
      - text: "About"
        href: about.qmd
```

## Step 4 — Add GitHub Actions

Create `.github/workflows/deploy.yml` to automate builds on push:

```yaml
name: Deploy
on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: quarto-dev/quarto-actions/setup@v2
      - run: quarto render
      - uses: peaceiris/actions-gh-pages@v4
        with:
          github_token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          publish_dir: ./_site
```

## Step 5 — Enable GitHub Pages

In your repository: **Settings → Pages → Source → gh-pages branch**.

Push to `main` and the site deploys automatically! 🎉

## Next Steps

- Add blog posts under `blog/posts/<post-name>/index.qmd`
- Add project pages under `projects/<project-name>/index.qmd`
- Customise colours in `styles.css`
